title: Nursery Rhyme. XXVIII. Historical
content:
Little General Monk
                Sat upon a trunk,
        Eating a crust of bread;
                There fell a hot coal
                And burnt in his clothes a hole,
        Now General Monk is dead.
                Keep always from the fire:
                If it catch your attire,
        You too, like Monk, will be dead.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. XXVIII. Historical", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
